name: Olive Leaf Extract
historicalUse: Olive is among the most medically significant plants in Mediterranean, Arabic, and Judaic traditions. Rambam devoted considerable attention to olive oil and olive preparations throughout his dietary writings, noting their effects on digestion, skin, and cardiac health. Ibn Sina classified olive leaf preparations for fever, inflammation, and wound healing. Olive leaves appear in the Ebers Papyrus (ancient Egyptian, ~1550 BCE) and throughout Greco-Roman botanical medicine.
modernEvidence: Olive leaf extract standardized to oleuropein has demonstrated antihypertensive, antimicrobial, and anti-atherosclerotic effects in clinical research. The OLIVUS-ex-vivo study and multiple RCTs show significant reductions in systolic and diastolic blood pressure comparable to captopril in mild-to-moderate hypertension. Oleuropein and hydroxytyrosol inhibit LDL oxidation, reduce endothelial inflammation, and exhibit antiviral activity.
mechanisms: ACE inhibition — oleuropein reduces angiotensin-converting enzyme activity
Calcium channel blockade — reduces vascular smooth muscle contraction
LDL antioxidation — hydroxytyrosol is among the most potent natural antioxidants identified
Anti-inflammatory — oleuropein inhibits NF-kB and reduces TNF-alpha, IL-6
Antiviral — interferes with viral protein synthesis and cell entry
benefits: Supports healthy blood pressure already within normal range
Supports healthy LDL profile and vascular integrity
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Well tolerated. Rare reports of mild headache at initiation (detoxification reaction described in some clinical reports).
contraindications: Caution in patients on antihypertensive medications — additive effect
Diabetes — monitor glucose as oleuropein has insulin-sensitizing properties
drugInteractions: Antihypertensive medications — additive blood pressure-lowering effect; monitor BP
Diabetes medications — additive glucose-lowering effect; monitor blood glucose
Anticoagulants — mild antiplatelet activity; monitor INR
physicianNotes: Well-evidenced antihypertensive and cardiovascular botanical. Consider in patients with prehypertension or as adjunct in mild hypertension alongside lifestyle modification. Monitor blood pressure when initiating in patients on antihypertensive therapy. Oleuropein content should be standardized for clinical reliability.
patientGuidance: If you take blood pressure medications, monitor your blood pressure when starting this formulation and inform your physician. A mild headache in the first week occasionally occurs and typically resolves.
